_meta.purpose: Reference characterization of phone sensors usable by an offline field biologist who CANNOT use the camera. Numbers are typical consumer-grade phone/MEMS ranges, not a single device datasheet. Each entry carries a confidence flag. Per project policy, treat these as engineering reference ranges; for a specific handset, override with the manufacturer datasheet before publishing measurements.
_meta.camera_status: DISABLED for this deployment. All ranging/dimension tasks route to LiDAR/ToF (infrared time-of-flight), which does not require RGB imagery.

sensors.name: Accelerometer
sensors.measures: Linear acceleration along 3 axes, including gravity (so it senses tilt at rest).

accuracy.best_case: Tilt/inclination +/-0.3 deg when static and calibrated
accuracy.typical: Tilt +/-1-2 deg; acceleration noise ~0.01-0.05 m/s^2 RMS
accuracy.worst_case: >3 deg under vibration or thermal drift

sensors.device_variation: Tilt accuracy varies ~2-3x between budget and flagship MEMS parts; bias shifts with temperature and after drops.
error_sources: Gravity vs. linear-accel ambiguity without gyro fusion
sensors.calibration: Lay phone flat on a level surface; zero the offset. Re-zero if readings drift after temperature change.

field_bio_use_cases.use: Level a microscope, balance, antenna mast, or camera trap mount
field_bio_use_cases.how: Read pitch/roll aloud; adjust until both near 0 deg

field_bio_use_cases.use: Slope/aspect of a sampling plot or stream bank
field_bio_use_cases.how: Lay phone on a rigid board across the slope, read incline

field_bio_use_cases.use: Detect equipment vibration / pump or centrifuge imbalance
field_bio_use_cases.how: Rest phone on the housing, watch peak acceleration
field_bio_use_cases.caveat: Useful for trend, not calibrated amplitude

sensors.voice_guidance: Ask the worker to lay the phone flat on the object and read the pitch and roll numbers. Confirm units. If numbers jump, ask them to hold still and re-read.

sensors.name: Gyroscope
sensors.measures: Angular velocity (rate of rotation) about 3 axes.

accuracy.best_case: Short-term rotation +/-0.5 deg over a few seconds
accuracy.typical: Heading drifts 1-10 deg/min from bias without fusion
accuracy.worst_case: Tens of deg/min drift when hot or uncalibrated

sensors.device_variation: Bias-instability differs widely; cheaper parts drift several times faster. Always fuse with accelerometer (and magnetometer) for stable orientation.
error_sources: Bias drift / integration error over time
sensors.calibration: Hold the phone still for a few seconds so the OS can estimate zero-rate bias before relying on rotation.

field_bio_use_cases.use: Measure turn/pan angle when sweeping a transect or antenna
field_bio_use_cases.how: Start still, rotate smoothly, read accumulated angle
field_bio_use_cases.caveat: Error grows the longer you integrate

field_bio_use_cases.use: Stabilize tilt readings on a moving boat/vehicle
field_bio_use_cases.caveat: Use the fused 'orientation' value, not raw gyro

sensors.voice_guidance: Tell the worker to hold the phone still first (lets it find zero), then rotate slowly. Read the fused orientation if available rather than raw rate. Warn that long, slow measurements drift.

sensors.name: Magnetometer (compass)
sensors.measures: Magnetic field vector -> compass heading (used with accel/gyro for absolute orientation).

accuracy.best_case: Heading +/-1-2 deg after figure-8 calibration, away from metal
accuracy.worst_case: >15-30 deg near vehicles, rebar, electronics, or magnets

sensors.device_variation: Hard/soft-iron offsets vary per device and per case (magnetic clasps wreck it).
error_sources: Declination (true vs magnetic north) not applied automatically
sensors.calibration: Wave the phone in a figure-8 a few times. Step away from gear, vehicles, and metal tables.

field_bio_use_cases.use: Record compass bearing of a transect, burrow entrance, or animal travel direction
field_bio_use_cases.how: Hold flat, away from metal, read heading; note magnetic vs true north
field_bio_use_cases.caveat: Apply local declination for true north

field_bio_use_cases.use: Re-orient a fixed plot or grid the same way each visit

sensors.voice_guidance: Have the worker do a figure-8 calibration and step away from metal before reading a bearing. Remind them to say whether they want magnetic or true north.

sensors.name: Barometer (pressure sensor)
sensors.measures: Atmospheric pressure -> relative altitude change and weather trend.

accuracy.best_case: Relative altitude +/-0.3 m over minutes (~0.04 hPa resolution)
accuracy.typical: Relative altitude +/-1 m; absolute altitude +/-5-10 m
accuracy.worst_case: Absolute altitude error >10 m as weather pressure drifts

sensors.device_variation: Resolution is similar across modern phones (~0.01-0.06 hPa); absolute accuracy needs a known reference altitude to anchor.
error_sources: Wind gusts / HVAC / car cabin pressure
sensors.calibration: Anchor at a known elevation (GPS or a benchmark) to convert pressure to absolute altitude; relative deltas need no calibration.

field_bio_use_cases.use: Elevation difference between two sample sites
field_bio_use_cases.how: Read at site A, walk to site B, read again; report the delta
field_bio_use_cases.caveat: Do both reads close in time so weather doesn't drift

field_bio_use_cases.use: Vertical position in a canopy/cliff/cave transect
field_bio_use_cases.how: Zero at base, climb, read delta
field_bio_use_cases.caveat: Avoid wind blasts on the port

field_bio_use_cases.how: Watch pressure trend over 1-3 h; falling fast = storm risk

sensors.voice_guidance: For elevation differences, have the worker read pressure at both points within the same half hour and report the difference, not absolute altitude. Flag a fast-falling trend as a weather/safety warning.

sensors.name: Ultra-Wideband (UWB) ranging
sensors.measures: Precise distance (time-of-flight) and angle-of-arrival between two UWB devices/tags.
sensors.range: Line-of-sight ~30-50 m; reliable ranging often <10-15 m

accuracy.worst_case: Multipath/NLOS errors of 0.5-2 m through bodies, foliage, walls

sensors.device_variation: Requires UWB hardware on BOTH ends (phone + UWB tag, or two phones). Not present on budget phones. AoA only on some models.
error_sources: Foliage and bodies absorbing the signal
Need for a paired UWB anchor/tag
sensors.calibration: Pair devices; if possible take one known-distance reference reading to check the offset.

field_bio_use_cases.use: Find a UWB-tagged trap, logger, or cached sample
field_bio_use_cases.how: Walk while reading range + direction to the tag
field_bio_use_cases.caveat: Foliage degrades it; expect to close the last meter by hand

field_bio_use_cases.use: Lay out precise short baselines / quadrat spacing
Geofence / spacing alerts around sensitive nests
field_bio_use_cases.how: Alert when range drops below a threshold

sensors.voice_guidance: First confirm BOTH the phone and the target have UWB. If only one side has it, fall back to LiDAR (short range) or GPS (coarse). Warn that foliage and going around objects inflates the distance.

sensors.name: LiDAR / Time-of-Flight (ToF) scanner
sensors.measures: Distance/depth to surfaces via infrared time-of-flight; produces ranges and a coarse depth map WITHOUT using the RGB camera.
sensors.range: ~0.1-5 m (degrades past ~5 m and in bright sun)
sensors.typical_sampling_rate_hz: Depth frames at 5-60 Hz on supported devices

accuracy.best_case: +/-1 cm at <1 m on a matte surface indoors
accuracy.worst_case: Fails on dark/specular/transparent/wet surfaces and in direct sunlight; errors >10 cm or dropouts

sensors.device_variation: Only some phones have a dedicated LiDAR/ToF emitter (e.g. Pro tiers). Where absent, there is NO camera-free depth option — fall back to UWB or a tape/known-object reference.
error_sources: Dark, shiny, transparent, or wet surfaces
sensors.calibration: Sanity-check against a known-length object (e.g. a 30 cm ruler) at the start of a session.

field_bio_use_cases.use: Measure plant height, burrow/cavity depth, log diameter, gap width
field_bio_use_cases.how: Aim at the surface, hold steady, read distance
field_bio_use_cases.caveat: Bright sun and shiny/wet surfaces break it

field_bio_use_cases.use: Microtopography / surface profile of a small plot
field_bio_use_cases.how: Sweep slowly to capture depth, note min/max

field_bio_use_cases.use: Standoff distance to a nest/animal without approaching

sensors.voice_guidance: Have the worker shade the target or work in shade, hold steady, and read the distance. Start the session by checking against a 30 cm ruler. If the phone has no LiDAR, switch to UWB or a tape/known-object method.

sensors.name: GNSS / GPS (supporting)

accuracy.best_case: Horizontal +/-1-2 m with dual-frequency GNSS, open sky
accuracy.worst_case: >10-50 m under canopy, in canyons, or near cliffs

sensors.device_variation: Dual-frequency (L1+L5) phones are markedly better under canopy than single-frequency.
error_sources: Vertical error large; prefer barometer for relative altitude
sensors.calibration: Let it settle 30-60 s for a fix; check reported accuracy radius before recording.

field_bio_use_cases.use: Geotag a sample, observation, or plot corner
field_bio_use_cases.how: Wait for the accuracy radius to shrink, then read coordinates
field_bio_use_cases.caveat: Canopy degrades it; pair with barometer for elevation

field_bio_use_cases.use: Anchor barometric altitude to an absolute elevation
field_bio_use_cases.how: Take GPS altitude in the open, then use barometer for deltas

sensors.voice_guidance: Have the worker wait for the accuracy radius to settle and read it aloud before recording coordinates. Note that altitude from GPS is weak; use the barometer for relative elevation.
